qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page describes how to troubleshoot common issues when using the cloud service to operate a virtual machine (VM, also known as an "instance"), including self-help solutions and advice for submitting support tickets.

qa_pairs.question: Can all cloud service issues be resolved by the user?
qa_pairs.answer: No, not all issues can be solved by the user; some things require a system administrator.

qa_pairs.question: When should I submit a support ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: You should submit a ticket if this guide advises you to, as it likely indicates an issue you cannot easily solve yourself.

qa_pairs.question: Why might I receive an "Invalid Credentials" error when attempting to log in to the cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: You will get an "Invalid Credentials" error if you have not specifically applied for and been granted a cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: How can I apply for a cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: You can apply for a cloud project using the CC cloud project and RAS request form available here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process after applying for a cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: After applying for a cloud project, it can take a few days for your request to be approved. When approved, you will receive an email with important information for accessing your project.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I haven't received my cloud project confirmation email after more than 3 business days?
qa_pairs.answer: Submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca including your name, institution, and the email address you used to submit the request.

qa_pairs.question: How do I determine which cloud to log into for my project?
qa_pairs.answer: Your confirmation email will tell you which cloud is hosting your project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find login links for different cloud services?
qa_pairs.answer: Login links for the different clouds can be found on the Cloud Wiki page in the "Using the Cloud" section.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have a confirmed cloud project but still cannot log in?
qa_pairs.answer: Check the System status page to see if there is an incident affecting service on your cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What username should I use to log in to the cloud service?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to use your username, the same as you would use to log in to an HPC cluster, and not your email address.

qa_pairs.question: How can I test if my login issue is related to my username or password?
qa_pairs.answer: You can test logging in at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/security/login to see whether it is an issue with your username or password.

qa_pairs.question: How do I reset my password if it is rejected?
qa_pairs.answer: You can reset your password by visiting this link: https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/security/forgot.

qa_pairs.question: If I still can't log in after following all troubleshooting steps, what information should I include when submitting a ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: Email cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your username, project name, which cloud you are trying to access, and a description of the steps you've taken so far.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about contacting support and submitting tickets?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about contacting support and submitting tickets can be found at the Technical Support page.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step if my virtual machine (VM) won't launch?
qa_pairs.answer: First, check to see if launching a VM would exceed any resource limits (also known as a "quota") for the number of VMs, CPUs, and GBs of RAM you can have in use at any given time.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check my project's resource limits or quotas?
qa_pairs.answer: Log in to your project cloud dashboard, and on the left-side navigation menu, click "Compute", then "Overview". It will show you how much of your allotted resources are currently in use.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I try to launch a VM that exceeds my allocated resources?
qa_pairs.answer: If you try to launch a VM that would cause you to exceed any of your resource limits, the launch will fail.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request more resources for my cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: You can request more resources for your project using this form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: What should I check if I receive the error message "Error: Failed to perform requested operation on instance "...", the instance has an error status: Please try again later [Error: No valid host was found. There are not enough hosts available.]"?
qa_pairs.answer: You should check if you may have chosen an inappropriate Availability Zone when trying to launch the instance.

qa_pairs.question: How can I resolve the "No valid host was found" error if it's due to an Availability Zone selection?
qa_pairs.answer: Fix this by setting the Availability Zone back to "Any Availability Zone" in the "Details" section when launching the instance.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I provide if I still encounter the "not enough hosts" error or need a specific Availability Zone when submitting a ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: Send an email to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca and provide the cloud name, project name, the steps you have taken thus far, and the reason why you need a particular availability zone if that is the case.
